title: RenodeServer: bidirectional connection for push messages
description:
Modify python/src/lichen/sim/renode_server.py to support unsolicited pushes to Renode.

Current: RenodeServer only reads requests and writes responses.
Needed: Also push RX_PACKET/RX_TIMEOUT when sim events fire.

```python
class RenodeServer:
    def __init__(self, ...):
        ...
        self._writer: asyncio.StreamWriter | None = None  # store for pushes
    
    async def _handle_connection(self, reader, writer):
        self._writer = writer  # save for push_to_renode()
        ...
    
    async def push_to_renode(self, data: bytes) -> None:
        """Push unsolicited message to Renode (called from sim callbacks)."""
        if self._writer is None:
            logger.warning("Cannot push: Renode not connected")
            return
        await _write_message(self._writer, data)
    
    def _on_rx_packet(self, payload: bytes, rssi: int, snr: int) -> None:
        """Callback from simulation when packet arrives."""
        asyncio.create_task(
            self.push_to_renode(encode_rx_packet(payload, rssi, snr))
        )
    
    def _on_rx_timeout(self) -> None:
        """Callback from simulation when RX times out."""
        asyncio.create_task(
            self.push_to_renode(encode_rx_timeout())
        )
```

Thread safety: callbacks run in sim context, push runs in asyncio. Use `call_soon_threadsafe` if sim runs in different thread, or keep both in same event loop.
acceptance_criteria: RenodeServer can push unsolicited messages; test shows RX_PACKET arrives at mock Renode client
close_reason: Added bidirectional push support to RenodeServer with _on_rx_packet/_on_rx_timeout callbacks
